summary: Gruber dismantles a Bloomberg article that relies entirely on investment bankers arguing Apple should hire investment bankers for large acquisitions. He points out the article offers zero evidence Apple has failed to acquire any company it pursued, and that its comparisons to Facebook, Google, and Microsoft assume more acquisitions are inherently good without examining outcomes. Gruber argues Apple's organic services growth and its successful direct-negotiation approach make the case against the article's thesis. He notes the Beats acquisition—done without bankers—produced Apple Music's 20 million subscribers, undermining claims that Apple needs outside help.
key_points: The Bloomberg article's only evidence that Apple 'struggles' with acquisitions is that they haven't made more of them—no failed bids are cited
Comparing Apple's acquisition count to Facebook, Google, and Microsoft assumes more and bigger deals are inherently good, without evaluating whether those acquisitions succeeded
Apple's services revenue is growing rapidly on its own, making a $75 billion Netflix acquisition unnecessary when licensing deals could achieve similar results
The Metaio anecdote—meant to show Apple's weakness—actually demonstrates Apple successfully acquiring companies on its own terms
The Beats deal, done without investment bankers, produced Apple Music with 20 million paying subscribers, contradicting the article's central thesis
money_quotes: The entire story consists of quotes from investment bankers arguing that Apple should hire investment bankers to make more large acquisitions. Really, that's it.
The only proof offered that Apple has struggled in any way to make any acquisitions is that they haven't made more acquisitions.
The comparison in the above paragraph completely rests on the assumption that more and bigger acquisitions are a good thing in and of themselves.
Even a $75 billion purchase of Netflix may not be enough. Who said that with a straight face? These fuckers wouldn't be satisfied unless Apple drunkenly spent every goddamn penny of their cash.
Translation: If only Apple had hired the investment bankers who fed this story to Bloomberg they could have bought Time Warner.
This anecdote really strengthens the case that Apple is doing itself a disservice by not hiring outside investment banks, and that the company suffers from 'inexperience in closing'. Not.
Apple's Beats acquisition suggests the opposite of this article's central thesis: Apple is better off not working with investment bankers even for a $3 billion deal.
key_insight: A Bloomberg article sourced entirely from investment bankers arguing Apple should hire investment bankers is a textbook example of self-serving industry advocacy masquerading as financial analysis.
